key_points: VP Ops spends 40hrs/month on manual LP reporting
Currently using Excel for fund administration
No formal budget allocated yet but CFO is aware of the pain
Tom mentions Sarah Chen as the 'person who signs off on anything over $50K'
action_items: Send case study on similar PE firm transformation

meddpicc.metrics: 40hrs/month spent on manual LP reporting across 3 funds
meddpicc.economic_buyer: Sarah Chen, CFO (mentioned, not yet engaged)
meddpicc.decision_criteria: Not yet defined — 'just want to see what's out there'
meddpicc.identify_pain: Manual reconciliation not scaling as AUM grows
meddpicc.champion: Tom Chen (VP Ops) — internal pain owner

full_transcript.speaker: Jessica Ryan
full_transcript.text: Thanks for the time Tom. What does your current fund administration workflow look like?

full_transcript.speaker: Tom Chen
full_transcript.text: We're basically running everything in Excel. I've got three separate workbooks for our three funds, and every month end I spend about 40 hours manually reconciling LP statements.

full_transcript.speaker: Jessica Ryan
full_transcript.text: That's significant. And as AUM grows, that's only going to get more complex.

full_transcript.speaker: Tom Chen
full_transcript.text: Exactly. Sarah — our CFO — she's been asking why we can't close books faster. I told her we need a proper system, but I need to show her something concrete.

full_transcript.speaker: Jessica Ryan
full_transcript.text: Has there been any budget conversation around this?

full_transcript.speaker: Tom Chen
full_transcript.text: Not formally. Sarah controls anything above $50K. I'd need her buy-in for a platform purchase. But she knows the pain — she's the one who complained about reporting delays at the last board meeting.

full_transcript.speaker: Jessica Ryan
full_transcript.text: Good to know. What else is on your radar besides solving this problem?

full_transcript.speaker: Tom Chen
full_transcript.text: We're also looking at our CSP for managed services. But the reporting thing is the one that keeps me up at night. If we had a demo to show Sarah, that would help move things forward.

key_points: Mark expressed frustration with manual reconciliation processes
Currently evaluating 3 vendors including Allvue
Decision expected Q3 2026
Budget allocated for new platform ($200K-$400K range)
Mark wants to see ROI comparison versus current Excel workflow

meddpicc.metrics: Manual reconciliation takes 40hrs/month per fund — Mark wants quantified ROI
meddpicc.economic_buyer: Mark Chen, CFO (present on call, engaged)
meddpicc.decision_criteria: Integration with existing Salesforce workflow, clear ROI payback period
meddpicc.paper_process: Budget approved informally, waiting for Q3 board review for final sign-off
meddpicc.identify_pain: Manual processes not scaling with AUM growth, board frustrated with reporting delays
meddpicc.champion: Mark Chen, CFO — confirmed pain owner and budget holder
meddpicc.competition: Allvue (pilot in progress), one other vendor

full_transcript.speaker: Jessica Ryan
full_transcript.text: Mark, thanks for making the time. Tom mentioned you've been thinking about automating the reporting workflow.

full_transcript.speaker: Mark Chen
full_transcript.text: Yes. The board is tired of waiting 3 weeks for quarter-end reports. We're a PE firm — our LPs expect real-time visibility, not Excel attachments.

full_transcript.speaker: Jessica Ryan
full_transcript.text: What's the impact of that delay?

full_transcript.speaker: Mark Chen
full_transcript.text: One of our LPs threatened to reduce allocation last quarter because they felt we were behind on reporting compared to our peers. That's a $50M mandate at risk.

full_transcript.speaker: Jessica Ryan
full_transcript.text: That's significant. Have you looked at any solutions yet?

full_transcript.speaker: Mark Chen
full_transcript.text: We're evaluating three vendors including Allvue. I need to see a clear ROI comparison — what's the payback period, how many hours saved, what's the implementation timeline.

full_transcript.speaker: Jessica Ryan
full_transcript.text: We can definitely provide that. What's the decision timeline looking like?

full_transcript.speaker: Mark Chen
full_transcript.text: We have a board review in Q3. If I can take a recommendation to them with a solid business case, I expect approval. Budget-wise, we're looking at $200K to $400K annually.

full_transcript.speaker: Jessica Ryan
full_transcript.text: And from a process standpoint — who else needs to be involved in the decision?

full_transcript.speaker: Mark Chen
full_transcript.text: I can make the call within that range with board backing. The key criteria for me are Salesforce integration — we're not adding another system that doesn't talk to CRM — and a clear ROI timeline under 12 months.

key_points: Marcus Webb (Head of Ops) is enthusiastic — used 'we need this' language unprompted
Marcus shared internal org chart showing reporting structure and decision flow
Integration with Salesforce is the #1 technical requirement
Tom defers to Marcus on technical evaluation
No next call scheduled with Sarah Chen (CFO)
action_items: Find way to get Sarah Chen on a call

meddpicc.metrics: Marcus estimates 500+ hours/year saved across ops team
meddpicc.economic_buyer: Sarah Chen, CFO (NOT present — not yet engaged despite Mark's 2-month-old promise)
meddpicc.decision_criteria: Salesforce integration quality, ease of implementation, API documentation quality
meddpicc.paper_process: Not discussed — rep said 'we'll figure out the process' when Marcus asked
meddpicc.identify_pain: Manual reporting creating operational bottleneck, ops team spending 25% of time on data entry
meddpicc.champion: Marcus Webb, Head of Ops — strong internal advocate, shared org chart, used 'we need this' language
meddpicc.competition: Allvue demo scheduled next week per Marcus

full_transcript.speaker: Jessica Ryan
full_transcript.text: Let me walk you through the Salesforce integration. Marcus, you mentioned that's the top priority?

full_transcript.speaker: Marcus Webb
full_transcript.text: Absolutely. If it doesn't sync with Salesforce, it's a non-starter. We're not adding another silo. Tom will back me up on this — we spent last year decommissioning three systems because of integration headaches.

full_transcript.speaker: Tom Chen
full_transcript.text: Marcus is right. Our CRM is the source of truth. Any platform we bring in needs to read and write deal data natively.

full_transcript.speaker: Jessica Ryan
full_transcript.text: Our platform has a native Salesforce connector. Let me show you how the bi-directional sync works.

full_transcript.speaker: Marcus Webb
full_transcript.text: This is exactly what we need. Seriously, this would save our ops team probably 500 hours a year. We need this.

full_transcript.speaker: Jessica Ryan
full_transcript.text: What does the decision process look like from here? Who else needs to see this?

full_transcript.speaker: Marcus Webb
full_transcript.text: We'll figure out the process. Let me share our org chart so you can see who reports to who.

full_transcript.speaker: Jessica Ryan
full_transcript.text: Great — and Sarah Chen, the CFO — has she seen any of this yet?

full_transcript.speaker: Marcus Webb
full_transcript.text: Not directly. Mark was going to brief her after our initial meeting. I'd say if we can show her the Salesforce integration and the ROI numbers, she'll be on board.

full_transcript.speaker: Tom Chen
full_transcript.text: Sarah is the key signature, but she delegates technical evaluation to Marcus and me. If we recommend it, she typically approves.

key_points: Marcus confirms they're leaning toward our solution vs Allvue
Allvue demo went well but Salesforce integration was 'clunky'
No progress getting Sarah Chen on a call — Marcus says 'she's busy till end of quarter'
Decision timeline slipping — originally Q3, now 'maybe September'
Marcus still a strong champion but can't unblock the economic buyer gap
action_items: Prepare exec summary for Sarah Chen

meddpicc.metrics: No formal ROI document delivered yet
meddpicc.economic_buyer: Sarah Chen, CFO (still not engaged — 3 months into process)
meddpicc.decision_criteria: Still focused on integration — no broader business case presented
meddpicc.paper_process: No procurement steps mapped — process is 'we'll figure it out'
meddpicc.identify_pain: Pain confirmed but no quantification tied to dollar impact
meddpicc.champion: Marcus Webb — strong but can't access economic buyer
meddpicc.competition: Allvue still in consideration, decision not finalized

full_transcript.speaker: Marcus Webb
full_transcript.text: Hey Jessica, wanted to give you an update. We had the Allvue demo last week.

full_transcript.speaker: Jessica Ryan
full_transcript.text: How did it go?

full_transcript.speaker: Marcus Webb
full_transcript.text: Honestly? Their Salesforce integration was clunky. Your platform is cleaner. The ops team is leaning your direction.

full_transcript.speaker: Jessica Ryan
full_transcript.text: That's great to hear. Have you had a chance to loop Sarah in yet?

full_transcript.speaker: Marcus Webb
full_transcript.text: I tried. She's swamped — quarter-end close. I sent her a summary but she hasn't responded. She said maybe September she can look at it properly.

full_transcript.speaker: Jessica Ryan
full_transcript.text: September? That's a slip from the original Q3 timeline.

full_transcript.speaker: Marcus Webb
full_transcript.text: I know. I'm pushing. But without her eyes on it, I can't get budget approval. She's the one who signs the checks.

full_transcript.speaker: Jessica Ryan
full_transcript.text: Is there any way we could put together a one-pager for her? Executive summary with ROI numbers?

full_transcript.speaker: Marcus Webb
full_transcript.text: That would help. If you can quantify the hours savings in dollar terms — Sarah responds to numbers. She's an Excel person at heart.

full_transcript.speaker: Jessica Ryan
full_transcript.text: Consider it done. I'll have something for you by end of week.

key_points: Sarah wants API-first platform for custom dashboard integration
Current stack doesn't support real-time data processing
Looking to replace Tableau with embedded analytics solution
Timeline: technical evaluation in June, decision July
action_items: Schedule technical deep-dive with engineering team

meddpicc.economic_buyer: CTO with CEO sign-off required above $100K
meddpicc.decision_criteria: API quality, data freshness, ease of integration
meddpicc.paper_process: No formal process yet — early stage evaluation
meddpicc.identify_pain: Slow dashboards causing missed insights during investor calls
meddpicc.champion: Sarah Kim, CTO — technical pain owner

full_transcript.speaker: Tom Rivera
full_transcript.text: Sarah, tell me what's driving the evaluation. What's not working with your current setup?

full_transcript.speaker: Sarah Kim
full_transcript.text: Tableau is fine for static reporting, but our investors now expect real-time dashboards. Our data pipeline takes 5+ minutes to refresh. In a board meeting, that's an eternity.

full_transcript.speaker: Tom Rivera
full_transcript.text: So the latency is creating visibility gaps during investor conversations?

full_transcript.speaker: Sarah Kim
full_transcript.text: Exactly. We had an investor call last month where a key metric was 7 minutes stale. We quoted the wrong number. Embarrassing.

full_transcript.speaker: Tom Rivera
full_transcript.text: Who else is involved in the decision beyond you?

full_transcript.speaker: Sarah Kim
full_transcript.text: I lead the technical eval. For anything above $100K, I need CEO sign-off. But if the engineering team and I recommend it, he'll approve.

full_transcript.speaker: Tom Rivera
full_transcript.text: What's your timeline look like?

full_transcript.speaker: Sarah Kim
full_transcript.text: Technical evaluation through June. We want to make a decision in July. We're looking at Tableau alternatives and also evaluating Looker.

full_transcript.speaker: Tom Rivera
full_transcript.text: Do you have a budget range in mind?

full_transcript.speaker: Sarah Kim
full_transcript.text: Not finalized yet. That's part of what we'll figure out post-evaluation. Your pricing would need to be competitive with Tableau's enterprise tier.
